title: pleopeltis subecostata
intro: Pleopeltis subecostata, also known as the subcostate polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions due to its antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis subecostata are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water, creating a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
